findings.summary: Treated GOALS.md as a writable execution-plan document and overwrote the stable project goals before the user corrected it.
findings.evidence: User correction: "Why did you overwrite goals? it was the stable goals for the project.... the plan should be another file?" The work had to be undone and moved out of /Users/alien/Projects/genomics/docs/GOALS.md.
findings.proposed_fix: Add a hard rule: GOALS.md and other strategy files are read-only unless the user explicitly asks to revise goals. Tactical plans must go in dated plan files, never strategy docs.

findings.summary: Implemented an explicit backward-compat layer for sample-scoped stage paths, then deleted it after the user clarified backward compatibility was not wanted.
findings.evidence: Compat symbols were added across analysis_loaders.py, case_bundle_builder.py, and generate_dashboard.py, then removed only after the user said "We do not need backward compat ... refactors should make the new thing work throughout... why backward compat?"
findings.proposed_fix: Before writing compatibility code during refactors, probe the actual on-disk corpus for legacy layouts. If the data is already canonical and the user did not ask for migration support, forbid compat scaffolding and go straight to deletion.

findings.summary: Declared the refactor effectively complete on partial gates instead of running the repo's top-level validation target immediately.
findings.evidence: After multiple focused suites and canaries passed, the user still had to say "run it". The later `just validate` run surfaced two real remaining issues: stale anchored facts in CLAUDE.md and then a smoke QC failure in scripts/pipeline_qc_smoke.py.
findings.proposed_fix: For any request phrased as execute the full plan, run the highest-level repo validation target before claiming completion. If only partial gates were run, state that explicitly and do not present the work as done.

findings.summary: Stayed in planning/documentation mode too long after the user had shifted to execution mode, which created avoidable plan churn and delay.
findings.evidence: The user had to re-anchor execution repeatedly: "Execute the full entire plan .. use common sense", "Well execute the full plan ... whats the hold up?", and later forced the final deletion cut on backward compatibility. Multiple plan/audit docs were revised before the decisive end-state change landed.
findings.proposed_fix: Add an execution-mode rule: once the user switches from planning to execution, stop writing new plan content unless a blocker or decision boundary appears. Only update audit docs after code and validation are done.
